title: Fix async score-processor dependency backfill handling
description:
Async score-processor-lambda does not consistently surface dependency-backed metadata/results for parent score extraction, diverging from sync API behavior.

Scope:
- Add shared dependency-aware single-target extraction helper in plexus.utils.scoring
- Use it in score-processor-lambda handler
- Treat dependency-unmet as terminal FAILED (no retry) using ScoringJob status
- Preserve success queue payload contract
- Add focused tests for subset selection, dependency-unmet terminal handling, fallback extraction, and retry semantics for true failures

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
Implementation started.

Plan of action:
1) Add shared helper in plexus.utils.scoring to run single-target scoring with dependency backfill and robust target extraction.
2) Integrate helper into score-processor-lambda/handler.py process_job path.
3) Map dependency-unmet outcomes to terminal FAILED without re-raise/retry.
4) Add/extend unit tests for subset_of_score_names usage, fallback extraction, dependency-unmet handling, and non-dependency error retry behavior.
5) Run targeted test suites and record outcomes.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
Implemented remediation on branch bugfix/async-dependent-score-metadata.

Code changes:
- Added dependency-aware single-target scoring helper in plexus/utils/scoring.py:
  - SingleScoreExecutionOutcome dataclass
  - score_single_target_with_dependencies(...) with subset_of_score_names targeting, robust target extraction (id -> parameter name/key fallback), list normalization, and dependency-unmet classification for SKIPPED outcomes.
  - Added DEPENDENCY_UNMET_MESSAGE constant for canonical terminal handling.
- Updated score-processor-lambda/handler.py process_job path to:
  - Resolve target score name from resolve_score_id response
  - Use score_single_target_with_dependencies(...)
  - Mark dependency-unmet outcomes as terminal FAILED without retry/re-raise
  - Avoid ScoreResult creation + response queue publish for dependency-unmet
  - Preserve existing retry behavior for non-dependency failures
- Added focused tests:
  - tests/test_scoring_single_target_helper.py (subset targeting, fallback extraction, skipped/dependency-unmet handling)
  - tests/lambda/test_score_processor_item.py (helper integration, dependency-unmet terminal path, non-dependency re-raise)

Validation:
- PASS: pytest -q tests/lambda/test_score_processor_item.py tests/test_scoring_single_target_helper.py
- NOTE: Existing score-processor-lambda/tests/test_handler.py fails in this environment due missing runtime deps (boto3/gql/botocore), unrelated to the new logic.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Final: implemented and validated async dependency-aware scoring extraction in score-processor-lambda. Dependency-unmet now maps to terminal FAILED without retry, with no ScoreResult/response queue emit. Added focused tests for helper behavior and lambda integration; targeted test suite passed.
